subject: First Year Algebra
instructions: Answer 4 questions. Write your solutions clearly in complete English sentences. You may quote results (within reason) as long as you state them clearly.

problems.text: Prove that the following polynomials are irreducible in \(\mathbb{Q}[x]\), stating clearly any theorems you wish to apply.

Show that the ring \(\mathbb{Z}[\sqrt{-2}]\) is a Euclidean domain with respect to the norm \(N(a+b\sqrt{-2})=a^2+2b^2\). (You may regard this ring as a subring of the field of complex numbers, with \(\sqrt{-2}=i\sqrt{2}\).)
Let \(R\) and \(S\) be commutative rings with 1 and \(\phi:R\to S\) a ring homomorphism with \(\phi(1)=1\). Let \(I\) be an ideal of \(S\) and \(\phi^{-1}(I)=\{r\in R\mid \phi(r)\in I\}\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Show that \(\phi^{-1}(I)\) is an ideal of \(R\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Show that if \(I\) is a prime ideal then so is \(\phi^{-1}(I)\).

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: If \(I\) is maximal, is \(\phi^{-1}(I)\) necessarily maximal? Give a proof or counterexample.

problems.text: Let \(R\) be a ring with 1 and \(M\) a (unital) left \(R\)-module.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: State what it means for \(M\) to satisfy the Ascending Chain Condition (i.e. for \(M\) to be Noetherian).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that if \(M\) satisfies the ACC then \(M\) is finitely generated.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Give an example of a finitely generated \(R\)-module \(M\) that does not satisfy the ACC.

problems.text: Use the rational canonical form to find one representative of each conjugacy class of elements of order 6 in \(\mathrm{GL}(4,\mathbb{Q})\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: If \(|F:K|=n\) and \(|E:F|=m\), where \(m\) and \(n\) are finite, show that \(|E:K|=nm\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that if \(|E:K|\) is finite, then \(|F:K|\) and \(|E:F|\) are both finite.
